purpose: Capture resource-aware telemetry and placement evidence, restart the selected LocalAI deployments, wait for readiness, and capture post-redeployment evidence before the official measurement window.

telemetryPriming.reason: Allow the monitoring agent to refresh resource telemetry annotations before triggering a new scheduling decision.

redeploymentPolicy.reason: Delete and recreate selected pods serially so that each replacement is scheduled from current telemetry without creating temporary surge pods on capacity-constrained variants.

governance.documentationPolicy: This profile is operational configuration and not repository documentation.
governance.schedulerBoundary: This procedure does not move running pods directly; it deletes selected pods in a controlled serial order so that the owning Deployment recreates them and the configured scheduler makes fresh placement decisions without introducing surge replicas.
governance.measurementBoundary: Benchmark measurements must start only after the post-redeployment capture and stabilization period have completed.
governance.comparisonPolicy: The same telemetry priming and redeployment procedure is used across default and load-aware variants so scheduler mode remains the controlled variable.
governance.annotationBoundary: CPU and memory annotations are required for the controlled rescheduling gate; disk-throughput and network-throughput annotations produced by provider-managed mon-agent are captured as optional evidence when available.
governance.scenarioScopePolicy: When a resource-aware-scheduler scenario config is supplied, target namespaces and expected LocalAI deployments are derived from that scenario so that one-, two- and three-tenant variants are handled without stale namespace targets.
governance.capacityBoundary: The serial pod recreation strategy avoids temporary maxSurge pods and is suitable for dense variants where resource requests leave insufficient allocatable memory for RollingUpdate surge replicas.
